extraInstructions:
Look for Figure Drawing classes. Note:
- Cost shows "$3 tuition" but total is ~$7 with model fee
- Extract instructor name if shown

address: 200 N Arthur Ashe Blvd, Richmond, VA 23220
extraInstructions:
Look for figure drawing classes in the Studio School listings.
These are usually instructed classes, so use tags: ["instructed", "nude"]

Look for events with "Figure Drawing" or "Drink & Draw" in the title.
These are usually open sessions.

Look for events or classes related to figure drawing or life drawing.
Check their events/classes page if there is one.

Look for figure drawing sessions and life drawing events.
They may link to Eventbrite for registration - if so, follow the links to get event details.

Search for figure drawing events in Richmond, VA.
For each event:
- Click through to get full details if needed
- Get the direct Eventbrite event URL
- Get the venue name and address from the event details

Look for figure drawing or life drawing sessions. They host a recurring nude figure drawing series (18+ only) on the last Wednesday of every month from 7:45-10pm. Tickets are required and sold through their website. Note the 18+ age restriction.

Look for the Figure Drawing Open Studio event. It runs on the last Thursday of every month from 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM, $10 at the door, ages 18+, no registration required. Check the events page for upcoming dates and confirm the session is still active.

Look for 'Drink & Draw RVA' events hosted by Brit Austin. These are monthly costumed (not nude) live model drawing sessions, typically on the 3rd Thursday of the month from 6-8PM. Admission is $15 and includes one drink and art materials. Note: some sessions may be held at alternate venues like Gallery5 (gallery5arts.org/calendar) — check both if needed.

Look for figure drawing and portrait drawing workshops. This studio focuses on Classical Realism methods and offers intensive multi-day workshops with live models covering figure, portrait, anatomy, and drawing. Workshops may be sporadic rather than recurring — capture any upcoming figure or portrait drawing workshops with dates, times, cost, and instructor.

Look for 'Queer Life Drawing' events hosted by RVA Thriving Artists. These are periodic figure drawing sessions with live queer-identified models in a community cafe event space. Check their events page and any calendar or upcoming events section. Sessions typically run about 2 hours in the evening with a $10 suggested donation. Also check their Instagram (@goldlionrva) or Facebook for event announcements if the website calendar is sparse.
